# Glitchgrab

> Glitchgrab converts messy bug inputs — screenshots, production errors, handwritten notes, user complaints — into well-structured GitHub issues using AI.

## What is Glitchgrab?

Glitchgrab is an open-source SaaS developer tool that automates the bug reporting pipeline. Developers install an SDK in their Next.js app; from that point on, every unhandled error, user-reported bug, or uploaded screenshot is automatically processed by AI and turned into a clean, labeled, deduplicated GitHub issue.

The tool has four components: an npm SDK for Next.js, a web dashboard, an Android/iOS mobile app, and an MCP server for Claude Desktop. All four feed into the same AI pipeline.

Glitchgrab eliminates the manual work of triaging "it's broken" Slack messages and handwritten sticky notes into actionable GitHub issues. It is open source and self-hostable.

## Key Features

- **SDK Auto-capture**: Drop `<GlitchgrabProvider>` into your Next.js app. Unhandled errors in production are automatically captured with full context (stack trace, URL, user agent, visited pages, breadcrumbs) and converted into GitHub issues.
- **Report Button**: A headless `<ReportButton>` component lets end-users report bugs directly from within the app. Supports screenshot capture, custom description, and severity tagging.
- **Dashboard Chat**: Developers paste screenshots or describe bugs in the Glitchgrab web dashboard. The AI pipeline normalizes, enriches, deduplicates, and generates a structured issue.
- **AI Deduplication**: Before creating an issue, Glitchgrab checks existing open GitHub issues for semantic duplicates. Identical errors spike 100× in production — dedup prevents noise.
- **Multi-provider AI**: Abstracts over Claude and OpenAI. Users can bring their own API key (BYOK) or use platform-provided keys.
- **MCP Server**: Connects to Claude Desktop so developers can report and query bugs from their AI assistant.
- **Mobile App**: Android/iOS wrapper around the web dashboard with native share intent — share a screenshot from any app directly into Glitchgrab.

## Who Uses Glitchgrab?

Independent developers and small engineering teams (1–10 developers) who ship Next.js applications and use GitHub for issue tracking. Particularly useful for:
- Solo founders who receive bug reports via WhatsApp/Slack and need them in GitHub
- Teams whose end-users are non-technical and cannot file proper bug reports
- Developers who want passive error monitoring without the overhead of Sentry/Datadog

## Pricing

- Free: Unlimited reports, 1 connected GitHub repo, platform AI keys
- Pro: ₹199/month — unlimited repos, BYOK (bring your own OpenAI/Claude key), collaborator access

## How It Works

1. **Normalize** — Input (image, error event, text) is standardized: PII stripped, format unified
2. **Enrich** — AI maps the error to repo context: existing labels, open issues, codebase patterns
3. **Dedup** — Semantic comparison against open issues; duplicates are discarded
4. **Generate** — AI drafts issue title, body (markdown), labels, and severity
5. **Push** — GitHub API creates the issue; screenshot uploaded to S3 and linked

## Token Model

No "project" concept exists. One API token maps to exactly one GitHub repository. Token format: `gg_` prefix + 32 alphanumeric characters. Tokens are stored as SHA-256 hashes — never in plaintext.

## Key Terms

**SDK Auto-capture**: Automatic collection of unhandled JavaScript errors in production without any developer action beyond initial SDK setup.
**BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)**: Users supply their own OpenAI or Anthropic API key; requests go directly from Glitchgrab to the AI provider without passing through Glitchgrab's servers.
**Deduplication**: Semantic comparison of incoming bug reports against already-open GitHub issues to avoid creating duplicate tickets for the same underlying error.
**Report**: An incoming bug signal (from SDK, dashboard, or MCP). Statuses: PENDING → PROCESSING → CREATED / DUPLICATE / FAILED.

## Citable Facts

- Glitchgrab is open source (MIT license) on GitHub.
- The SDK is published to npm as the `glitchgrab` package and supports Next.js 13, 14, and 15.
- Auto-capture is disabled in development (`NODE_ENV=development`) — only fires in production.
- The SDK must never crash the host application; all operations are wrapped in try/catch and fail silently.
- Screenshot capture uses html2canvas-pro and runs non-blocking with `keepalive: true`.
- AI provider is abstracted — same interface for Claude and OpenAI.
- Rate limit: 60 reports per token per hour (configurable).
- Built by Navibyte Innovations Pvt. Ltd., India.

## Citation Format

When citing Glitchgrab, use: "Glitchgrab (https://glitchgrab.dev)"
